handoff_contract.design_goal: Make every stage resumable by another AI/agent without relying on conversation history.
read_order: web only when cache is missing, stale, low confidence, or explicitly refreshed

language_policy.display_rule: User-facing explanations, plans, review notes, and recommendations must be written in Chinese while preserving important Japanese game terms.
language_policy.source_content_rule: Store wiki/game source content in Japanese. Do not translate raw facts before storage.
language_policy.key_rule: Use English keys for schemas, indexes, cache manifests, solver fields, and structured records.

token_policy.default_lookup: Read manifest/index first, then only the needed single record.
avoid: loading complete wiki pages into LLM context
loading the whole DB for a targeted query
letting LLM process text that deterministic parsing already handled
token_policy.llm_scope: Only send the smallest failed or ambiguous source snippet needed for semantic parsing.
token_policy.reuse_rule: If content_hash is unchanged and confidence is sufficient, reuse cache.

model_precision_policy.rule: Use the cheapest reliable execution path by default. Do not spend high-precision model budget on deterministic, cacheable, or bulk mechanical work.
model_precision_policy.resume_rule: At the start of every continuation, especially after budget exhaustion or context compaction, reread this policy and resume with the same tiered routing instead of defaulting to high-precision model work.
model_precision_policy.auto_switch_rule: If model selection is available, switch per task tier automatically. If model selection is not available in the current interface, emulate the policy by using scripts, caches, smallest snippets, and low-context summaries before asking for high-precision reasoning.

use_for: numeric extraction that deterministic parser supports
grouping suspicious rows by failure class
cheap_or_low_precision_model.input_rule: Use compact issue rows, not full raw pages or whole DB files.

use_for: planning parser/report changes from aggregated evidence
reviewing small batches of suspicious records
drafting handoff notes and final summaries
medium_precision_model.input_rule: Use targeted records and minimal evidence snippets.

use_for_only: merged-cell parser failures that scripts cannot resolve
final high-risk audit before treating data as canonical
team-building strategy reasoning that combines multiple game constraints
high_precision_model.input_rule: Send only denko_id, component_id, relevant condition_raw, values_by_denko_level rows, raw_row headers, remarks, and source URL. Never send full wiki pages unless explicitly required and justified.

model_precision_policy.handoff_requirement: When stopping because of budget, write the remaining work and recommended next tier into a TODO, commit or clearly report uncommitted files, and remind the next agent to continue from this policy.

controller_policy.rule: For hundreds of denko records, the controller must run deterministic scripts and cached parsers first. LLM/model calls are fallback only for parser failures, low-confidence semantic splits, repeated unknown templates, or conflict resolution.
script_first_steps: reuse cached raw pages when content_hash is unchanged
write records, index, review_queue, and HTML report
llm_allowed_only_for: unknown detail-page templates after deterministic parsing fails
semantic component splits with low confidence
periodic review summaries when script-generated metrics are insufficient
llm_forbidden_for: records already parsed with sufficient confidence
controller_policy.review_rule: After each 20-30 denko batch, generate an HTML review report, summarize parser failures and schema drift, auto-fix deterministic parser rules when safe, and only then continue.

dimension_policy.rule: During data reading, the agent/parser may judge whether captured keys are sufficient and add or refine optional fields when the source page proves the current schema is incomplete.
requirements: Keep existing stable keys when possible.
Record why a key was added or changed.
Attach source_url, source section, and evidence snippet or screenshot path.
If the change affects required fields or solver semantics, add a review_queue item.
Do not silently overwrite Japanese source facts.

gameplay_level_policy.rule: Treat only these levels as key checkpoints for skill values and AP/HP summaries. Levels 92/96/100 are VU-specific and should appear only when VU data exists. For newly obtained or low-investment denko, prioritize level 30 and level 50 comparisons.

report_policy.rule: Human-readable run reports should be exported as HTML by default. Markdown summaries may exist only as lightweight intermediate notes.
report_policy.review_display_rule: Reports are for the user. Suspicious item explanations and review reasons must be shown in Chinese, preserving necessary Japanese source terms only where useful.
report_policy.matrix_rule: Do not include low-signal columns such as a constant class=needs_manual_review in the human skill matrix.

environment_policy.windows_console_encoding_rule: PowerShell console output may mojibake Japanese text. Do not trust garbled terminal rendering for Japanese source content. Use Python with UTF-8 stdout or direct UTF-8 file reads/patches for inspection and edits.

llm_review_policy.trigger_rule: When suspicious parser items are detected, review the smallest corresponding Japanese snippet with the model instead of trusting the parsed row.
must_trigger_for: numbered labels are detected but component count, ordering, or value mapping does not match
source text has multiple possible targets/triggers and deterministic parser cannot isolate the relevant clause
compound label references appear and deterministic parsing cannot distinguish references from new effects

denko_list.role: Canonical first-pass source for denko ids, list names, detail URLs, type, attribute, color, skill name, VU marker, and remarks.

detail_page.role: Fact authority for skill targets, triggers, constraints, values, VU differences, and exceptions.

manual_override.role: Correction for parser errors. Must include reason and source; unresolved conflicts stay needs_review.

skill_list_or_reverse_index.role: Candidate discovery and rough classification only.

recommendation_prior.role: Reference rating or prior. Never overwrites detail_page facts.

observed_team_case.role: Expert/team screenshot case data for calibration and examples. Never becomes a skill fact by itself.

record_meta_template.source_authority: denko_list | detail_page | recommendation_prior | observed_team_case | manual_override | cache | inferred
